tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible chronic low glucose. Elevated creatinine and chloride, low CO₂, and abnormal urine findings (proteinuria, ketonuria, bilirubin, low specific gravity, brown color, ammoniacal odor) may indicate renal impairment and possible dehydration or metabolic disturbance. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend clinical correlation for renal function, metabolic status, and further evaluation of urine abnormalities.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
